meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Solving Version Control for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Learn how to use SaaS starter kits with Antigravity and Claude Code to master version control and ship faster for your clients today.
content.answer_block: The lack of version control is a silent career killer. By adopting a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you integrate Git workflows, automated deployments, and environment management by default. This transition forces professional standards, eliminates manual deployment errors, and ensures your client projects remain scalable, secure, and easily maintainable from day one.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: 3 AM, a client project is due, and you realize you just overwrote your only production-ready file. The sheer panic of losing hours of work because you didn't have a safety net is the baptism by fire every freelancer faces. Without version control, you aren't building software; you're just gambling with your reputation.

But the era of chaotic, manual file management is over. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you shift from 'coding on the fly' to 'engineering at scale.' This guide pulls back the curtain on how to utilize Antigravity and Claude Code to automate your infrastructure, secure your commit history, and deliver professional-grade SaaS products with zero friction.

steps.title: Initialize with a Robust Starter Kit
steps.description: Start with a foundation that includes Git, ESLint, and environment variable management to prevent configuration drift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready SaaS boilerplate that includes TypeScript, Tailwind, Supabase integration, and a pre-configured .gitignore file.

steps.title: Automate Branching Strategy
steps.description: Establish a clear workflow where main is production and feature branches handle development to ensure no code is pushed without review.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting and unit tests on every pull request to the main branch.

steps.title: Configure Environment Isolation
steps.description: Use environment variables to separate staging from production, preventing accidental data corruption during client demos.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a secure environment loading utility that handles development, staging, and production secrets via .env.vault.

steps.title: Implement Automated Snapshots
steps.description: Ensure your code state is committed before any major architectural change using AI-assisted commit messages.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that triggers a git commit with a descriptive AI-generated message after every successful feature completion.

steps.title: Final Deployment Validation
steps.description: Verify your build integrity before pushing to production to ensure your client receives a polished, bug-free application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a pre-deploy check script that verifies build success, environment variable presence, and test coverage thresholds.

pro_tips: Always use conventional commits (feat:, fix:, chore:) to make your Git history readable for future developer handoffs.
Never hardcode API keys; use a secret management service like Doppler or Vercel Secrets to keep your code repo clean.
Implement a 'preview environment' for every PR so your clients can test features live before you merge to production.
Set your IDE to 'auto-save' and pair it with a git-watcher extension to minimize the risk of losing uncommitted work.
Use semantic versioning (SemVer) tags on your releases so you can instantly roll back to a known stable version if a bug appears.
content.next_steps: After completing these steps, push your new repository to GitHub, link it to a Vercel or Netlify project for auto-deployments, and begin your first feature sprint using Claude Code to keep your documentation in sync with your logic.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop building in the dark and start shipping with absolute confidence. Stop manually managing files and start managing products. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
